title: Implement MeshCore app message ingress into LICHEN local interface
description: Why this exists: app compatibility should allow MeshCore clients to inject local messages into LICHEN without changing RF protocol. What needs to be done: decode MeshCore client send-message operations, validate destination/group/channel semantics for the supported subset, translate to LICHEN local message/CoAP or internal send API, and return compatible acknowledgements/errors. Acceptance criteria: tests send a MeshCore app message and observe the expected LICHEN local message call; unsupported destinations/groups return explicit errors; payload length/encoding limits are enforced; no MeshCore RF frame is transmitted over LoRa.
notes:
Implemented no-hardware MeshCore message ingress slice on 2026-07-01: added lichen_app_interface_submit_text() with single-owner submit provider semantics and stats; MeshCore adapter handles SEND_CHANNEL_TXT_MSG for channel 0/plain UTF-8 text via submit_text callback, validates malformed payloads and UTF-8, requires tx_free ACK preflight before submit side effects, maps callback errno to MeshCore errors, and returns OK only after submit succeeds. Direct SEND_TXT_MSG now has deterministic no-hardware behavior: payloads shorter than 6-byte peer prefix return ERR_ILLEGAL_ARG, invalid UTF-8 returns ERR_ILLEGAL_ARG, well-formed unknown peer prefix returns ERR_NOT_FOUND. Gateway MeshCore adapter forwards channel text to app_interface submit_text with broadcast metadata (to UINT32_MAX), blocks same-thread reentrant MeshCore egress during command dispatch with ERR_BAD_STATE, and preserves cross-thread egress by blocking behind command ACK. Canonical vectors/generator updated to 33 source vectors / 27 adapter vectors; docs updated for SEND_CHANNEL_TXT_MSG no-provider/provider split. Production /msg/local send provider remains out-of-scope and tracked by project-LICHEN-t2hn.7.2; without a submit provider, production MeshCore channel text deterministically returns ERR_UNSUPPORTED_CMD rather than pretending to send RF.

Validation evidence: local jq/generator parity PASS (meshcore_app_compat.json 33/33 vectors match generate.py) and git diff --check PASS. EC2 i-05e27e074db786e22 clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-9c4y6, Zephyr v3.7.0, SDK 0.16.8: direct native_sim app_interface PASS 12/12; direct native_sim meshcore_adapter PASS 20 passed, 1 expected skip, total 21; direct native_sim meshcore_gateway_adapter PASS 13/13. Final Twister command: west twister -T lichen/tests/app_interface -T lichen/tests/meshcore_adapter -T lichen/tests/meshcore_gateway_adapter -p native_sim --inline-logs --outdir twister-out-ingress --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-9c4y6/lichen. Result PASS: 4/4 configurations, 66 test cases executed, 3 expected skips, 0 warnings, 0 failed, 0 errored.

Review evidence: multiple review rounds found and fixed P1/P2 issues: duplicate submit providers, gateway deadlock/interleaving, missing UTF-8 validation, submit before ACK capacity, direct SEND_TXT_MSG unsupported instead of deterministic not-found/illegal-arg, generator parity drift, cross-thread egress regression, and missing tx_free preflight. Final three independent codereview passes (Planck the 2nd, Newton the 2nd, Laplace the 3rd) reported no P0/P1/P2 findings on the current diff.
close_reason: Completed: MeshCore channel text ingress now maps to the shared app-interface submit_text boundary with channel/type/UTF-8 validation, required ACK preflight before submit, deterministic direct SEND_TXT_MSG errors, gateway reentrancy/cross-thread protections, canonical vector/generator/doc updates, direct native_sim and Twister evidence, and three final clean codereview passes.
